evals.prompt: I want to add Google and GitHub login to my Next.js + FastAPI app. Users should be able to sign in without creating a password.
evals.expected_output: Agent asks: do you want a managed provider or roll your own OAuth? Recommends Clerk for vibe-coders. Sets up ClerkProvider in layout.tsx, middleware.ts for route protection, UserButton component, and FastAPI JWKS-based JWT verification endpoint.

evals.prompt: I set up Clerk but when a new user signs up, I don't have their data in my PostgreSQL database. How do I sync them?
evals.expected_output: Agent implements Clerk webhook: sets up POST /webhooks/clerk route with svix signature verification, handles user.created event to insert into users table with clerk_id, handles user.deleted. Shows DB schema addition (clerk_id column, nullable, unique, indexed).

evals.prompt: My FastAPI backend needs to know who the current user is when the Next.js frontend makes API calls. How does Clerk work across the stack?
evals.expected_output: Agent explains the full flow: Next.js gets session token from Clerk -> sends as Bearer token in API calls -> FastAPI get_current_user_id() verifies JWT using Clerk JWKS endpoint -> returns clerk user ID. Shows both the frontend fetch with auth header and the FastAPI Depends() pattern.
